mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior 1-3 months lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional returns in periods where FEDFUNDS falls for 2 consecutive months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the central bank's tolerance, the market prices tighter policy and lower real valuations, producing negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses sign consistency across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which later pressures earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after 0.3pp 3-month unemployment increases, or if the effect is not detectable in at least 2 out of 3 major slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deterioration in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market break that is stronger than either series alone; this combination predicts negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risk is repriced.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not outperform each single-series signal, or if positive 20-day returns occur in more than half of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise in RSAFS relative to its recent trend signals softer consumer demand and weaker revenue momentum, which should weigh on equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in most RSAFS contraction episodes, or if the conditional return spread versus non-contraction months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO together with accelerating HOUST indicates improving real activity and future cyclical earnings, which supports positive SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-up and HOUST-up condition fails to produce positive average 60-day returns, or if its predictive power disappears after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary labor regime, a falling PAYEMS reading translates quickly into weaker aggregate income and risk appetite, so the next 5 trading days of SPX returns are negative after payroll deterioration.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by at least 150k in a month
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward SPX returns are not negative following payroll drops of 150k or more, or if the effect vanishes once unemployment is already elevated.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth remains positive while retail sales and housing starts both weaken, the economy is rotating from goods demand to a softer cyclical path; that combination should produce negative SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-positive / retail-sales-negative / housing-negative configuration does not predict weaker 60-day SPX returns than GDP alone, or if the sign of the conditional return is positive across multiple rolling windows.
